outcomes.statement: files dropped into the inbox are picked up and ingested
records that fail validation are rejected without poisoning the rest of the batch
duplicate records (within a batch and against prior batches) are not loaded twice
valid, de-duplicated records are loaded into the warehouse, and re-running the same input does not double-load
when a run fails, a human is alerted with enough context to act

requirements.description: the target warehouse table(s) plus the supporting tables: a dedupe index and a quarantine/rejects store
detect/poll the inbox, claim the set of files for a batch, and move them out of the inbox so they are not picked up twice
read each claimed file and parse it into records, tolerating a malformed file without aborting the batch
validate each record against the schema/rules; route failures to quarantine and pass the good ones on
persist rejected records with the reason so a bad record is set aside, not lost, and does not poison the batch
a persisted index/key of records already seen across prior runs, used to detect cross-batch duplicates
drop duplicates within the batch and against the dedupe index, keeping one copy
a deterministic natural/business key per record that the load uses to upsert (the basis of idempotency)
upsert/merge the de-duplicated records by load key inside a transaction so re-running the same input does not double-load
a record of each batch run (files, counts, status) so a retried run is recognized and the load can be made idempotent at the batch level
on a failed run, notify a human (channel/email) with the batch id, failing stage, and error context
the driver that sequences the stages, owns the batch transaction boundary, and decides what counts as a failed run
a failed/partial run can be safely retried from a clean state without double-loading or losing quarantined records

concerns.reason: the plan is explicitly batch (a run processes a set of files end to end); building streaming/continuous ingest infrastructure would be scope drift

mustHaves.statement: dropped files are picked up and ingested
bad records are rejected without poisoning the batch
duplicates within and across batches are not loaded twice
re-running the same input does not double-load
a failed run alerts a human

requiredEdges.why: parsing operates on the files the watcher has claimed for the batch
you can only validate records once they are parsed
validation routes failing records into the quarantine store
the quarantine/rejects table is part of the schema
only validated records are carried forward to be de-duplicated
cross-batch de-dupe checks records against the persisted dedupe index
the dedupe index is a persisted table defined in the schema
de-dupe is keyed by the same natural/business key the load upserts on
the load consumes the de-duplicated record set
idempotent upsert/merge is performed on the load key
the load writes into the warehouse table
a successful load records the loaded keys into the dedupe index for future cross-batch de-dupe
batch-level tracking lets a retried batch be recognized and not re-loaded
the orchestrator detects a failed run and triggers the alert
orchestration starts by claiming the batch via the watcher
orchestration sequences through to the load and owns the transaction boundary
orchestration opens/closes the batch record that tracks the run
safe retry keys off the recorded batch state to resume cleanly
retry relies on the load being idempotent so re-processing does not double-load
